name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
overview: RAG retrieves relevant chunks from a local document index and injects them into a prompt to ground LLM answers in your data. Use when the LLM lacks domain knowledge or you need citations.

steps.name: Ingestion and Chunking
steps.what: Parse source documents and split text into overlapping chunks for indexing.
steps.decision: Baseline: chunk_size=512, overlap=10%. Increase overlap to 20% for dense technical text where a fact can span a sentence boundary. Decrease chunk size for Q&A over short structured data.
failure_points: Chunk boundaries split sentences mid-fact — the retriever finds the chunk but the answer is in the adjacent half.
PDF extraction misses tables and inline equations — extract these separately or use a structured PDF parser.

steps.name: Embedding
steps.what: Encode each text chunk into a dense vector using a sentence embedding model.
steps.decision: BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5 for accuracy. Downsize to gte-large or enable ONNX quantization if latency per query exceeds 50ms.
failure_points: Using a different embedding model at query time than at index time — produces incompatible vector spaces and zero relevant hits.
Embedding model max token length (typically 512) silently truncates long chunks, losing the tail of the text.

steps.name: Vector Storage
steps.what: Store chunk embeddings in a vector database for approximate nearest neighbor search.
steps.decision: FAISS for single-machine in-memory search. ChromaDB for persistent local storage with metadata filtering. Qdrant for multi-tenant cloud deployments with built-in payload filtering.
failure_points: No versioning on the vector store — stale document embeddings persist after content updates and return outdated answers.
Missing metadata (source file, chunk index, timestamp) makes it impossible to verify or cite retrieved passages.

steps.name: Query Embedding and Retrieval
steps.what: Embed the user query using the same model and retrieve top-k similar chunks.
steps.decision: Start with top-k=5. Increase to 10-20 for long-form synthesis queries. Normalize query embeddings if the embedding model uses cosine similarity.
failure_points: Query and index embeddings normalized differently — cosine similarity scores are unreliable when only one side is L2-normalized.
Retrieval returns semantically similar but topically wrong chunks when the query term is polysemous (e.g. 'bank' → river bank vs. financial bank).

steps.name: Reranking
steps.what: Score retrieved chunks against the query with a cross-encoder to improve relevance ordering before generation.
steps.decision: Add a reranker when retrieval precision drops below 70% on held-out queries. Keep reranker top-n at 3-5 to avoid exceeding the LLM context window.
failure_points: Reranker top-n too high — all retrieved chunks fit in context but the LLM ignores the later ones due to attention sink effects.
Reranker latency not measured — cross-encoders can add 100-500ms per query at large top-k, bottlenecking the entire pipeline.

steps.name: Generation
steps.what: Inject retrieved context into a structured prompt template and call the LLM to produce a grounded answer.
steps.decision: Use a strict prompt template that separates CONTEXT from QUESTION with explicit delimiters. Limit injected context to 3000 tokens to leave room for the system prompt and answer.
failure_points: Hallucination when the LLM ignores the provided context and answers from parametric memory — add an explicit instruction: 'Answer only from the context above. If unsure, say so.'
Context window overflow from combined prompt + retrieved chunks — silently truncates the system prompt or the question itself.

evaluation_checks: Retrieval precision@5 >= 0.70 on a held-out question set
Answer groundedness: no claims in the answer absent from the retrieved context
End-to-end latency <= 2s for typical 5-chunk retrieval + generation
common_failure_points: Chunk boundaries splitting sentences across semantic units, causing retrieval to miss half of a critical fact.
Using a different embedding model at query time than at indexing time, producing incompatible vector spaces and zero relevant hits.
Retrieving outdated document versions because the vector store lacks a versioning or timestamp filter.
Hallucination when the LLM ignores the provided context and falls back on parametric knowledge due to a weakly structured prompt.
Context window overflow because the combined prompt and retrieved chunks exceed the LLM's maximum token limit, causing silent truncation of the system prompt.
